records.ety: [L. terror, akin to terrere to frighten, for tersere; akin to Gr. <?/ to flee away, dread, Skr. tras to tremble, to be afraid, Russ. triasti to shake: cf. F. terreur. Cf. Deter.]

defs: Extreme fear; fear that agitates body and mind; violent dread; fright.
That which excites dread; a cause of extreme fear.
quotes: Those enormous terrors of the Nile.
Rulers are not a terror to good works.
There is no terror, Cassius, in your threats.
notes: ☞ Terror is used in the formation of compounds which are generally self-explaining: as, terror-fraught, terror-giving, terror-smitten, terror-stricken, terror-struck, and the like.
